steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a punctuation question testing restrictive vs. non-restrictive appositives. The blank fills "as ______ attest." The proper names "Roberto Scandone and Christopher Kilburn" identify which researchers are meant, making them a restrictive appositive — which takes no commas.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "researchers, Roberto Scandone and Christopher Kilburn,": Two commas set the names off as non-restrictive, implying they are merely supplemental information. But the names are essential to identifying the specific researchers, so commas are incorrect here. ✗
  • "researchers, Roberto Scandone and Christopher Kilburn": One comma before the names creates a mismatched pair — a non-restrictive appositive must be enclosed in commas on both sides. This is also incorrect. ✗
  • "researchers Roberto Scandone and Christopher Kilburn": No commas. A restrictive appositive that identifies which researchers must not be set off by commas. This is grammatically correct. ✓
  • "researchers Roberto Scandone, and Christopher Kilburn": The comma after "Scandone" illogically separates the two coordinated names in the appositive, breaking the phrase. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
